target.title: fix(web-ui): eliminate streaming markdown flicker — avoid full DOM replacement during token updates
target.body:
## Summary - **Problem:** During streaming, every coalesced token update triggers a full DOM replacement because `unsafeHTML(toSanitizedMarkdownHtml(markdown))` re-renders the entire subtree. This causes visible flicker: paragraphs blink, code fences disappear and reappear, and long responses visibly “wink” on each update. - **Why it matters:** Any skill producing multi-paragraph markdown or code fences (e.g., InvestorClaw stonkmode) appears broken during streaming. The UI feels unstable and distracts users from reading as content arrives. - **What changed:** When `isStreaming` is true, the renderer now uses a plain‑text `
` with `white-space: pre-wrap`, avoiding markdown HTML generation and preventing Lit from replacing the entire DOM subtree. When streaming completes, the component switches back to full markdown rendering. - **What did NOT change:** The block-streaming coalescer (`DEFAULT_BLOCK_STREAM_COALESCE_IDLE_MS = 1000`) remains unchanged. No changes were made to markdown sanitization, message grouping, or post-stream rendering. ## Change Type - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes #68655 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause - **Root cause:** `renderGroupedMessage()` uses `unsafeHTML(toSanitizedMarkdownHtml(markdown))` on every render cycle. Because the markdown string changes on every token, Lit replaces the entire DOM subtree each time. - **Missing detection / guardrail:** No streaming-aware rendering path existed to avoid full HTML regeneration during partial markdown states (e.g., unclosed code fences). - **Contributing context:** Unclosed code fences (` ``` `) produce unstable intermediate HTML (`

`, partial `

`), amplifying the flicker when the closing fence arrives.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level that should have caught this:**
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- **Target test or file:** A streaming markdown integration test in `ui/tests/chat/streaming-markdown.spec.ts`.
- **Scenario the test should lock in:**  
  Streaming multi-paragraph markdown should append text without removing previously rendered paragraphs.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:**  
  Only a streaming integration test can detect DOM replacement flicker; unit tests cannot observe DOM stability across Lit render cycles.
- **Existing test that already covers this:** None.
- **If no new test is added, why not:**  
  This PR focuses on the minimal safe fix; test coverage can follow in a separate PR.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Streaming markdown now displays as plain text (with preserved newlines) until streaming completes.  
- Once streaming finishes, the final message renders with full markdown formatting.  
- Flicker is eliminated; paragraphs remain stable as new tokens arrive.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[streaming token arrives] -> [unsafeHTML regenerates full HTML] -> [DOM subtree replaced] -> [visible flicker]

After:
[streaming token arrives] -> [plain-text 
 updates text only] -> [DOM stable] -> [no flicker]
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS + Linux (OpenClaw dev environment)
- Runtime/container: Web UI dev server
- Model/provider: Any streaming model
- Integration/channel: InvestorClaw skill (stonkmode)
- Relevant config: Default block-streaming coalescer (1000ms)

### Steps

1. Enable stonkmode: `/portfolio stonkmode on`
2. Trigger a long streaming response: `/portfolio holdings`
3. Observe streaming paragraphs and code fences.

### Expected

- Text appends smoothly with no flicker.
- Completed paragraphs remain stable.
- No full-frame blink when code fences open/close.

### Actual (before fix)

- Entire message container flickers on each token.
- Paragraphs disappear and reappear.
- Code fences cause full-frame flashes.

## Evidence

- [x] Screenshot/recording (manual verification)
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Perf numbers

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios:**  
  - Multi-paragraph streaming  
  - Streaming with unclosed code fences  
  - Streaming JSON fences (` ```json `)  
  - Short single-paragraph streaming  
- **Edge cases checked:**  
  - Directionality (`dir=`) preserved  
  - Switching from streaming → final markdown  
  - No regression in non-streaming messages  
- **What you did NOT verify:**  
  - No automated test coverage yet  
  - No mobile browser testing

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes  
- Config/env changes? No  
- Migration needed? No  

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Plain-text fallback means markdown formatting is not visible during streaming.  
  **Mitigation:** Final render still uses full markdown; this is a temporary streaming-only state.

- **Risk:** Some custom markdown behaviors may expect HTML during streaming.  
  **Mitigation:** No known features rely on mid-stream HTML; markdown is only required at final render.

---

# **Code Changes Included in This PR**

### **CSS**

```css
/* Streaming mode: plain text rendering to avoid DOM replacement flicker.
   Uses pre-wrap to preserve newlines while allowing normal word breaking.
   Switches to full markdown rendering when streaming completes. */
.chat-text--streaming {
  white-space: pre-wrap;
}
```

### **TypeScript**

```ts
opts.isStreaming
  ? html`

      ${markdown}
    
`
  : html`

      ${unsafeHTML(toSanitizedMarkdownHtml(markdown))}
    
`;
```
target.author: michaelatamuk
